rulings.Case Title: HARRY LEMASTER et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 29, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corporation.¿s motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issues 1, 2, 4 and 5 (general negligence, strict liability design and manufacturing defect, civil battery and punitive damages) and taken off calendar as to issue 3 (false representation). Defendant satisfied its burden of production of making a prima facie case that it is entitled to prevail on its government contractor defense as to non-duty to warn claims and plaintiffs have not presented admissible evidence creating a triable issue on this defense. Defendant satisfied its burden of production that plaintiffs do not have and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with malice, fraud or oppression by clear and convincing evidence and plaintiffs have not presented admissible evidence creating a triable issue that they are entitled to punitive damages. Plaintiffs dismissed their claim for false representation. At the outset of the hearing the court will provide tentative rulings on the parties¿ evidentiary objections. If any party contests the tentative ruling, the hearing will be at 10:30am on September 30, not at 9:30am on September 29. Any party wishing to contest the tentative ruling must notify the court with a copy to opposing counsel by 4:30pm on September 29 by email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGEW H FRANKLIN VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDENTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION And Notice Of Motion To Quash Service Of Summons
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 29, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Lehigh Hanson, Inc.¿s motion to quash service of summons is granted. Plaintiff(s)¿s request for jurisdictional discovery is denied. Plaintiff(s) failed to make any showing of the second element of the alter ego doctrine ¿ i.e. ¿the facts are such that an adherence to the fiction of the separate existence of the corporation would, under the particular circumstances, sanction a fraud or promote injustice¿ (9 Witkin, Summary of California Law (10th ed. 2005) ¿Corporations¿ §10, quoting Minifie v. Rowley (1921) 187 Cal. 481, 487)) ¿ or that any of the jurisdictional discovery sought to be taken will produce evidence to prove that element. (See also Sonora Diamond Corp. v. Superior Court (2000) 83 Cal. App. 4th 523, 539 (alter ego doctrine not a grounds to impose personal jurisdiction where there was no evidence of any wrongdoing by either the subsidiary or the parent or any evidence of injustice flowing from the recognition of the subsidiary¿s separate corporate identity)). The most recent supplemental papers filed by plaintiff(s) merely show that plaintiffs, if entitled to do so under Civil Code section 3295, desire to take punitive damages discovery from Lehigh Hanson, not that any fraud or injustice would result from adhering to the fictional separate existence of Lehigh Hanson from Kaiser Gypsum. Plaintiff(s)¿s desire to take punitive damages discovery from Lehigh Hanson can be fully accomplished through means other than invoking the alter ego doctrine. If any party contests the tentative ruling, the hearing will be at 10am on September 30, not at 9:30am on September 29. Any party wishing to contest the tentative ruling must notify the court with a copy to opposing counsel by 4:30pm on September 29 by email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SHARON WOODY et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Re-Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 29, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Co., Inc.¿s motion for judgment on the pleadings is granted. No opposition filed. Hearing required unless counsel for the moving party informs the plaintiffs of the tentative ruling and they say they do not wish to contest. = (220/HEK)
